name: Student Transportation
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA / Ridesta), owned by CDPQ (controlling) and Ullico (minority) since April 2018
principals: Howard Anderson Jr listed in source data as Owner — appears to be stale/incorrect; STA-Waterford is an STA operating yard, not an independent company
districts_or_customers: Connecticut operations across Danbury, Wilton, Groton, Naugatuck, Ledyard, Griswold, Guilford
Nationwide contracts across US and Canada
recent_news: Privatized in April 2018 in US$877M take-private led by CDPQ with Ullico; continues to expand via tuck-in acquisitions (Pacific Western Transportation, etc.).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate — this is Student Transportation of America, the #3 US school-bus contractor (22,000+ vehicles), owned by CDPQ/Ullico. The 'Waterford, CT — Howard Anderson Jr' entry in the source list appears to be a data-quality error reflecting STA's CT operations base at 20 Rope Ferry Rd. Could only be relevant as a strategic peer/competitor or seller of CT yards in a future divestiture; not a regional family-owned target. State='OR' in source data is inconsistent with CT HQ — flag for data cleanup.

name: Minnesota Coaches Inc
owner_or_parent: Tiger Infrastructure Partners (Fund IV) — growth investment closed Dec 2024 / STB approval Feb 2025. Operates under Reliant Transportation Group / Minnesota Coaches & Affiliates platform. Regan family retains operating role.
principals: Manfred Litzenberger (Manager — Minneapolis location per source)
districts_or_customers: 36 public school districts plus charter and private schools across Minnesota and Wisconsin
recent_news: December 2024: Tiger Infrastructure Partners announced growth capital investment in Minnesota Coaches and 6 affiliates (Marschall Line, Rehbein Transit, Ready Bus, Voyageur Bus, Minn-Dakota Coaches, Faribault Transportation). STB approval effective Feb 25, 2025. Northborne Partners advised sellers.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in — just recapped by Tiger Infrastructure (Dec 2024). Now itself a roll-up platform competing for Midwest tuck-ins. Could be relevant as a competitor in MN/WI sourcing or, longer-term, as a partial divestiture seller of individual affiliate yards. Track for competitive intel.

name: Butler's Bus Svc
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (Audax Private Equity portfolio); acquired via Van Pool Transportation LLC subsidiary, effective Dec 31, 2024
principals: John Lyskawa and Emo Chynoweth (acquired company in 2006; Emo Chynoweth now Beacon Mobility VP)
districts_or_customers: NH and VT school districts across 6 operating yards: Barnstead NH, Milford NH, North Haverhill NH, Orleans VT, Lyndonville VT, White River Junction VT
recent_news: January 2025: Beacon Mobility (Audax-owned) announced acquisition of Butler's Bus Service. STB filing November 2024; deal effective Dec 31, 2024. Brought Beacon to 28 companies in 25 states.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in — acquired by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) at end of 2024. 300-bus, 6-yard NH/VT footprint would have been an ideal Summit tuck-in but is now off the market. Useful as comp for Summit's NH/VT sourcing benchmarks; lost-deal intel.

name: School Transportation Sltns
districts_or_customers: Various public and private schools, universities, airports, hotels, senior care centers, and municipalities across central Alabama
recent_news: 2015 WBRC news coverage of a bus running a red light (minor safety incident); company has continued steady operation. No M&A or major news since.
fit_notes: STRONG tuck-in candidate. Independent, founder-led (Walter Turner, ~30+ years), ~250 employees, Birmingham/central AL footprint with diversified school + charter + special-needs revenue. Founder approaching natural succession horizon; no obvious next-gen successor identified. Anchor Southeast platform play for Summit; recommend prioritizing for outreach.

name: A M Bus Co
recent_news: No recent M&A or major press identified. Continues as established CPS contractor; member of CPS's roster of small/MBE bus contractors.
fit_notes: Possible tuck-in for a Chicago/IL platform — independent, woman-owned, ~100-bus CPS contractor with 30+ years of history. Smaller than Summit's core target size (revenue ~$3-5M) but valuable for CPS contract diversity / MBE certification. Founder-led with succession optionality. Note CPS contracting environment is highly relationship-driven and politically sensitive.
